title: Patna Schools Closed Till June 20 Due to Heatwave; DM Orders Suspension for Class 5 and Revised Timings for Higher Classes - Jagran Josh
full_text:
Patna Schools Closed Till June 20 Due to Heatwave; DM Orders Suspension for Class 5 and Revised Timings for Higher Classes
Patna schools will remain closed for students up to Class 5 till June 20 due to the ongoing heatwave. Check the latest DM order, revised timings for Classes 6-8, and important guidelines for parents and schools.
In Bihar, the Patna district administration has extended restrictions on school operations till June 20, 2026 due to heatwave and rising temperatures. The order has been issued by the District Magistrate (DM) to safeguard students from extreme weather conditions. According to the latest directive, all academic activities for students up to Class 5 will remain suspended until June 20, while classes for higher grades will continue with modified timings.
Patna School Holiday: Class-Wise School Schedule
As per the DM's order:
- Classes up to Class 5 in all government and private schools, including pre-schools and Anganwadi centres, will remain closed till June 20.
- Classes 6 to 8 may be conducted only up to 10:30 AM to protect students from peak daytime temperatures.
- Schools have been instructed to strictly follow the revised timings until further orders.
The administration stated that the decision was taken considering the health risks posed by the intense heatwave conditions across the district.
Why Was the Decision Taken?
Patna and several parts of Bihar have been experiencing soaring temperatures, prompting authorities to take precautionary measures for students' safety. The district administration noted that prolonged exposure to extreme heat could adversely affect children's health, leading to dehydration and heat-related illnesses.
The order aims to minimise students' exposure to harsh weather while ensuring continuity of education through adjusted schedules where feasible. The district administration will continue monitoring the weather situation and may issue further directions if heatwave conditions persist. The Patna District Magistrate has ordered the closure of classes up to Class 5 till June 20, while Classes 6 to 8 will operate only until 10:30 AM due to the ongoing heatwave.
Other States Where Schools Have Been Closed Due to Heatwave in June 2026
Apart from Bihar, several states across India have announced school closures or revised class timings due to the intense heatwave in June 2026.
- Uttar Pradesh: Several districts, including Prayagraj, Varanasi, and Mirzapur, have suspended classes for primary students or shifted schools to morning hours following soaring temperatures.
- Jharkhand: District administrations in heatwave-affected regions have ordered the closure of schools for younger students while advising institutions to conduct classes only during cooler morning hours.
- Odisha: Authorities in multiple districts have temporarily shut schools and Anganwadi centres as temperatures crossed 40°C, prioritising children's safety.
- Rajasthan: Many districts have revised school timings and suspended outdoor activities due to extreme daytime temperatures.
- Madhya Pradesh: Some districts have altered school schedules and reduced instructional hours after the India Meteorological Department (IMD) issued heatwave alerts.
State governments continue to monitor weather conditions closely and may extend closures or modify school timings further depending on the intensity of the heatwave.

Her reporting focuses on providing students, parents, and educators with accurate updates and practical insights on examinations, results, and policy changes.
